name: salt-ds-v1.43
description: Salt Design System v1.43.0 — initial baseline. 38 components from @salt-ds/core@1.43.0, real TypeScript prop interfaces, narrative facts (ADR, security model, theming guide), v1.43 release note.

attributes.summary: JPMorgan Chase's open-source enterprise design system — React component library and design tokens for building accessible, themeable UIs.

props.name: value
props.description: AccordionGroup value.

props.name: expanded
props.description: Whether the accordion is expanded.

props.name: defaultExpanded
props.description: Whether the accordion is expanded by default.

props.name: indicatorSide
props.description: Side to align the Accordion's indicator. Defaults to `left`.

props.name: onToggle
props.description: Callback fired when the accordion is toggled.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the accordion is disabled.

props.name: status
props.description: The status of the accordion.

props.name: name
props.description: The name that Avatar represents.

props.name: nameToInitials
props.description: Defines the function that gets initials. Default is capital first letter of each separate word in name. If a function is not passed or returns undefined, Avatar will default to Icon.

props.name: src
props.description: Image src of Avatar.

props.name: size
props.description: Multiplier for the base avatar.

props.name: fallbackIcon
props.description: Icon to be used as a default item. Defaults to `UserIcon`.

props.name: color
props.description: Changes Avatar's color.

props.name: value
props.description: The number to display on the badge. If `value` is not provided, the badge will render as a dot badge.

props.name: children
props.description: If a child is provided the Badge will render top right. By defualt renders inline.

props.name: max
props.description: The max number to display on the badge.

props.name: variant
props.description: Emphasize the styling by applying a background color: defaults to false

props.name: status
props.description: A string to determine the current state of the Banner

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the button will be disabled.

props.name: focusableWhenDisabled
props.description: If `true`, the button will be focusable when disabled.

props.name: appearance
props.description: The appearance of the button. Options are 'solid', 'bordered', and 'transparent'. 'solid' is the default value.

props.name: sentiment
props.description: The sentiment of the button. Options are 'accented', 'neutral', 'positive', 'negative' and 'caution'. 'neutral' is the default value.

props.name: loading
props.description: If `true`, the button will be in a loading state. This allows a spinner to be nested inside the button.

props.name: loadingAnnouncement
props.description: Text to be announced by screen readers, intended to be used in conjunction with the `loading` prop.

props.name: accent
props.description: If provided an accent is shown in the specified position.

props.name: disabled
props.description: **Deprecated:** Use the InteractableCard component instead If `true`, the card will be disabled.

props.name: hoverable
props.description: If `true` the card will have hover styling.

props.name: interactable
props.description: **Deprecated:** Use the InteractableCard component instead If `true`, interactive styles will be applied to `Card`. These styles give prominence to certain content on the page.

props.name: variant
props.description: Styling variant. Defaults to "primary".

props.name: checked
props.description: If `true`, the checkbox will be checked.

props.name: defaultChecked
props.description: Whether the checkbox component is checked by default This will be disregarded if checked is already set.

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the checkbox will be disabled.

props.name: error
props.description: **Deprecated**: Use validationStatus instead If `true`, the checkbox will be in the error state.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description: If true, the checkbox appears indeterminate. A data-indeterminate attribute is set on the input.

props.name: inputProps
props.description: Properties applied to the input element.

props.name: inputRef
props.description: Used to access the hidden `<input>` element.

props.name: label
props.description: The label to be shown next to the checkbox.

props.name: name
props.description: The name applied to the input.

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Callback when checkbox loses focus.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback when checked state is changed.

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Callback when checkbox gains focus.

props.name: value
props.description: The value of the checkbox.

props.name: validationStatus
props.description: Validation status, one of "warning" | "error" | "success" Checkbox has styling variants for "error" and "warning". No visual styling will be applied on "success" variant.

props.name: children
props.description: At least 1 children is expected, options are `ColumnGroup` or `GridColumn`.

props.name: zebra
props.description: If `true`, zebra stripes are enabled (odd/even rows have alternate colours)

props.name: hideHeader
props.description: If `true`, grid header is hidden.

props.name: columnSeparators
props.description: If `true`, column separators are rendered.

props.name: pinnedSeparators
props.description: If `true`, separators are rendered between pinned and unpinned columns.

props.name: rowData
props.description: Row data objects. Sparse arrays are supported.

props.name: rowKeyGetter
props.description: Should return unique string for a given row data object. If rowData is sparse then this function should work with undefined row data objects and create keys based on row index. `(row: T, index: number) => string`

props.name: defaultSelectedRowIdxs
props.description: Rows with these indices are selected by default.

props.name: selectedRowIdxs
props.description: Selected row indices for controlled mode.

props.name: variant
props.description: The variant to use. Options are `primary` and `secondary`. Default value is `primary`. `secondary` variant changes grid background to reduce contrast.

props.name: rowSelectionMode
props.description: Options are `single`, `multi` and `none`.

props.name: columnMove
props.description: If `true`, user will be able to move columns using drag and drop.

props.name: onColumnMoved
props.description: Accepts `(columnId: string, fromIndex: number, toIndex: number) => void`

props.name: cellSelectionMode
props.description: Options are `range` and `none`.

props.name: headerIsFocusable
props.description: If `true`, keyboard navigation is enabled for the header.

props.name: open
props.description: Display or hide the component.

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Callback function triggered when open state changes.

props.name: status
props.description: The status of the Dialog

props.name: initialFocus
props.description: Which element to initially focus. Can be either a number (tabbable index as specified by the order) or a ref. Default value is 0 (first tabbable element).

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the Dialog

props.name: disableDismiss
props.description: Prevent the dialog closing on click away

props.name: disableScrim
props.description: Prevent Scrim from rendering

props.name: idProp
props.description: Optional id prop Used for accessibility purposes to announce the title and subtitle when using a screen reader

props.name: orientation
props.description: The orientation of the divider. Defaults to `"horizontal"`.

props.name: variant
props.description: The variant of the divider. Defaults to `"primary"`.

props.name: position
props.description: Defines the drawer position within the screen. Defaults to `left`.

props.name: open
props.description: Display or hide the component.

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Callback function triggered when open state changes.

props.name: variant
props.description: Change background color palette

props.name: disableDismiss
props.description: Prevent the dialog closing on click away

props.name: disableScrim
props.description: Prevent Scrim from rendering

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the file drop zone will be disabled.

props.name: status
props.description: Status indicator to be displayed.

props.name: onDrop
props.description: Callback for on drop event

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the field will be disabled.

props.name: readOnly
props.description: If `true`, the field will be read-only.

props.name: id
props.description: Optional id prop Used as suffix of FormFieldLabel id: `label-{id}` Used as suffix of FormFieldHelperText id: `helperText-{id}`

props.name: necessity
props.description: Displays necessity on label

props.description: Validation status

props.description: The marker to use in an empty read only Input. Use `''` to disable this feature. Defaults to '—'.

props.description: Render prop to enable customisation of anchor element.

props.description: Either "default" or "never". Determines when underline should be applied to the link.

props.description: Callback function triggered when open state changes.

props.description: Set the placement of the Menu component relative to the trigger element. Defaults to `bottom-start` if it's the root menu or `right-start` if it's nested.

props.description: Function that returns a [virtual element](https://floating-ui.com/docs/virtual-elements). If this is provided, it will override MenuTrigger.

props.name: bordered
props.description: Styling variant with full border. Defaults to false

props.description: Set the default selected radio button in the group

props.name: disabled
props.description: Set the disabled state

props.name: error

props.name: inputProps
props.description: Props to be passed to the radio input

props.description: Used to access the hidden `<input>` element.

props.name: label
props.description: The label to be shown next to the radio icon

props.name: name
props.description: Name of the radio group

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Callback for blur event

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback for change event

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Callback for focus event

props.name: readOnly
props.description: Set the read only state. **Note**: Setting a standalone radio button as read-only is not accessible. The whole radio buttton group should be set as read-only instead.

props.name: value
props.description: Value of radio button

props.name: validationStatus
props.description: Validation status, one of "warning" | "error" | "success" RadioButton has styling variants for "error" and "warning". No visual styling will be applied on "success" variant.

props.name: fixed
props.description: If `true` the scrim is bound to the document viewport. The default value of this prop is false, and the default behavior is for Scrim to be bound to its parent container (nearest positioned ancestor).

props.name: open
props.description: If `true` the scrim is shown.

props.name: announcerInterval
props.description: Determines the interval on which the component will continue to announce the aria-label. Defaults to 5000ms (5s)

props.name: announcerTimeout
props.description: * Determines the interval after which the component will stop announcing the aria-label. Defaults to 20000ms (20s)

props.name: className
props.description: The className(s) of the component

props.name: completionAnnouncement
props.description: Determines the message to be announced by the component when it unmounts. Set to null if not needed.

props.name: disableAnnouncer
props.description: If true, built in aria announcer will be inactive

props.name: role
props.description: The prop for the role attribute of the component

props.name: size
props.description: Determines the size of the spinner. Must be one of: 'default', 'large', 'small', 'medium'.

props.name: id
props.description: The ids of the SvgSpinner components

props.name: children
props.description: The children will be the Tooltip's trigger.

props.name: hideArrow
props.description: Whether to hide the Tooltip arrow. Defaults to `false`.

props.name: hideIcon
props.description: Whether to hide the status icon within the Tooltip. Defaults to `false`. If no status is provided, icon will also be hidden.

props.name: content
props.description: Content displayed inside the Tooltip. Can be a string or a React component.

props.name: status
props.description: Optional string to determine the status of the Tooltip.

props.name: enterDelay
props.description: Delay in milliseconds before the Tooltip is shown.

props.name: leaveDelay
props.description: Delay in milliseconds before the Tooltip is hidden. Defaults to 300ms.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Option to not display the Tooltip. Can be used in conditional situations like text truncation. Defaults to 0.

props.description: Option to remove the hover listener.

props.name: disableFocusListener
props.description: Option to remove the focus listener.

facts.title: Overview
facts.body: Salt is an open-source enterprise design system developed by JPMorgan Chase. It ships a React component library plus design tokens (colour, density, corner radius, typography) themed for both light and dark modes. Salt targets data-dense financial interfaces and ships with WCAG 2.1 AA conformance. Components are tree-shakeable from @salt-ds/core; theming is applied at the <SaltProvider> boundary.

facts.title: Architectural decision: React, not Web Components
facts.body:
# Status
Accepted (2025-08-12)

# Context
Enterprise consumers run a mix of React, Angular, and internal frameworks. A framework-agnostic Web-Components layer was considered to maximise reach.

# Decision
Ship React-first. The team's expertise + the cost of shadow-DOM-aware theming and the lack of native form-association support outweigh the cross-framework upside. We will not block framework-agnostic wrapper packages built by consumers.

# Consequences
Non-React apps integrate via render-into-element patterns or community wrappers. The team owns React + Tokens; the community owns the bridges.

facts.title: Security model
facts.body: Salt is a client-rendering library; it never makes outbound network calls of its own, never reads/writes local storage by default, and depends on zero runtime-only third-party trackers. CSP-compatible: all styling is via class names against `<style>` blocks generated at build time. No inline `<script>` or runtime `eval()`. Trusted Types compatible — no innerHTML usage. Components MUST receive untrusted text via standard React children; consumers handle HTML-sanitisation upstream if they need it.

facts.title: Theming guide
facts.body: Wrap your app in `<SaltProvider density={...} mode={'light'|'dark'}>`. All components inherit from the provider; nested providers override. Custom themes: extend the default token set via a CSS variable layer (see `@salt-ds/theme`). Density: `low` / `medium` / `high` / `touch` (touch added in v1.45).

facts.title: v1.43.0 release notes
facts.body: v1.43.0 — Initial 1.x stable line documented in this demo. Components ship from @salt-ds/core with WCAG 2.1 AA conformance. Tree-shakeable exports; theming via <SaltProvider density={...} mode={'light'|'dark'}>.
